steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: Rising G.I. Joe prices had a "counterintuitive" effect on demand. Normally, higher prices reduce demand — but here the opposite happened: new buyers who previously had no interest entered the market. The blank describes what rising prices did to demand. The word must mean to draw out or call forth new demand that did not previously exist.

steps.title: Test each choice against that meaning
steps.content:
Substitute each word and check whether it captures the idea of rising prices drawing new buyers into the market:
  • "satisfying": fulfilling existing demand — this means meeting wants that were already there, not generating new buyers who weren't previously interested. ✗
  • "capitalizing": taking advantage of an opportunity — this describes what a buyer does, not what rising prices did to demand itself. ✗
  • "exploiting": using something for gain — like capitalizing, this describes a buyer's action, not the mechanism by which prices pulled new buyers into the market. ✗
  • "eliciting": drawing forth or calling out — rising prices elicited (called out) demand from buyers who had not previously wanted the toys. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
